vulnerabilities.description: Login accepts default test credentials (test:test), confirmed by successful 302 to /home with a valid session cookie.
Directory listing is enabled, exposing /static/templates/*.html and other assets that reveal internal routes and template logic.

vulnerabilities.type: Local file inclusion (constrained to static dir)
vulnerabilities.description: The error template uses {% include error_type %} with user-controlled input. Including arbitrary files within the static directory is possible; traversal outside is blocked (400), but design enables template/file inclusion from /static which could be abused if attacker-controlled files are present.
Forms do not contain CSRF tokens, enabling cross-site request forgery for authentication and profile changes.

Profile image upload accepts multipart files with no visible client-side restrictions. If server-side validation is weak, may allow uploading arbitrary files to /static/images leading to stored XSS or file overwrite.
Session cookie is set with HttpOnly but without Secure and SameSite attributes, increasing exposure to session theft (over HTTP) and CSRF.
